Title: Male fertility: A case of enzyme identity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Old yellow enzyme family / 12-oxophytodienoate reductase isozyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: OPDA reduction step in jasmonate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxophytodienoic acid at the 10,11 double bond in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical required step for jasmonate synthesis in anthers; essential step established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: oxylipin plant hormone / lipid-derived signaling metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes male fertility, including timely anther dehiscence, filament elongation, and pollen function; also implicated in defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OPR3 abolishes jasmonate synthesis required for male fertility, causing delayed anther dehiscence, reduced filament elongation, and mostly inviable or nonfunctional pollen; exogenous jasmonate restores fertility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OPR3 was identified genetically as the defective gene in a jasmonate-rescuable male-sterile mutant. 2. OPR3 is the Arabidopsis isozyme that efficiently reduces 9S,13S-OPDA and is required for jasmonate synthesis in anthers. 3. Mutation of OPR3 causes male sterility with delayed dehiscence and short filaments, and fertility is restored by jasmonate or wild-type OPR3 cDNA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutant analysis; jasmonate rescue; 12-OPDA rescue comparison; cDNA complementation; RNA in situ hybridization; in vitro enzyme activity evidence cited; phenotypic analysis of anther dehiscence and fertility
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all floral organs before male meiosis; later in petals, stamens and pistil; also expressed in vegetative organs including leaves; not detectable at the time of anther dehiscence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in floral organs before male meiosis, later in petals, stamens and pistil, and in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate jasmonate production and male fertility for hybrid seed production or fertility control, but may affect defense-related jasmonate responses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Male sterility when defective; possible effects on jasmonate-mediated defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also independently identified as delayed dehiscence1 (dde1). OPR3, unlike OPR1 and OPR2, efficiently reduces the naturally relevant 9S,13S-OPDA stereoisomer. Review article summarizing results from primary studies.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Fatty acid desaturases required for trienoic fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Trienoic fatty acid supply to jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Linolenic acid biosynthesis / precursor supply module
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Fatty acid precursors for trienoic fatty acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Trienoic fatty acids including linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyze formation of trienoic fatty acids, including linolenic acid, which serves as the precursor of jasmonate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: oxylipin plant hormone / lipid-derived signaling metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes male fertility and defense responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The fad3 fad7 fad8 triple mutant produces almost no trienoic fatty acid, is male sterile, and can be rescued by exogenous linolenic acid, jasmonate, or 12-OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD3, FAD7 and FAD8 function redundantly to produce trienoic fatty acids including linolenic acid. 2. Loss of all three genes causes male sterility while photosynthesis remains normal. 3. Fertility rescue by linolenic acid, jasmonate, and 12-OPDA shows that these genes act upstream of jasmonate biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant analysis; exogenous linolenic acid rescue; jasmonate rescue; 12-OPDA rescue
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous linolenic acid; exogenous jasmonate; exogenous 12-OPDA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to alter jasmonate precursor supply and fertility, but strong effects on reproductive development make deployment mainly useful for fertility control rather than nutritional enhancement
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The article summarizes prior work. These genes are discussed as upstream jasmonate-biosynthetic genes but are not the central new discovery of the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonate response/signaling controlling male fertility and defense-related gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Leaf storage protein gene expression; jasmonate-responsive fertility-related processes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mediates jasmonate-dependent activation of downstream responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential signaling component for jasmonate action in male fertility; mutants phenocopy jasmonate-deficient sterility and are not rescued by jasmonate
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: oxylipin plant hormone / lipid-derived signaling metabolite
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes male fertility and defense responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: coi1 mutants are male sterile and insensitive to jasmonate treatment, indicating a signaling defect downstream of jasmonate biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COI1 is required for normal jasmonate-mediated male fertility. 2. coi1 mutants are male sterile with phenotypes similar to opr3/dde1. 3. Unlike jasmonate-biosynthetic mutants, coi1 cannot be rescued by exogenous jasmonate, distinguishing signaling from biosynthetic defects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; exogenous jasmonate non-rescue; comparison with biosynthetic mutants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for fertility control, but disruption is expected to compromise jasmonate responses including defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Male sterility; impaired defense response mediated by jasmonate
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a comparator showing that signaling mutants cannot be rescued by jasmonate. In this review it is contextual rather than the main enzyme-identity focus.
